Notice Of Motion For Relief Pursuant To C.C.P. 2030.290(A)
Off Calendar Jan-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Keith J. Ferrara, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: E. M. McCartt
Kevin Greenquist Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Pensco Pension Services, Inc.

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff E. McCartt's motion for relief from waiver of objections is granted. The Court notes that the objections, other than based on the attorney client or attorney work product privilege, would be overruled. In addition, while the responses suggest some factual information, the requests seek facts and not merely conclusions. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Its 1st Document Requests
Off Calendar Jul-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:

Kathryn A. Schofield, Esq., for E.M. McCartt, Plaintiff/Opposing Party
Kevin P. Greenquist, Esq., for Pensco Pension Services, Inc.'s Defendant/Opposing Party

Hearing Held: Temtative Ruling Adopted: Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to 1st Document Requests granted in part, denied in part, as follows:
a. Denied as to Requests no. 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14 - 18 [inclusive], 20, 22, 24, 26, 28, 30, 32, and 34;
b. As to Requests no. 23, 31 and 33, plaintiff shall serve further verified responses within 10 days of the date of this order identifying any responsive documents that are not subject to the attorney-client or attorney work product privilege; and shall produce legible copies of all such documents as an attachment to the further responses;
c. As to Requests no. 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 19, 21, 25, 27, and 29, plaintiff shall produce for inspection and copying the originals of all documents [except those subject to the attorney-client or attorney work product privilege] identified in plaintiff's response to those requests; production shall be conducted within 10 days of the date of this order. COMM. PAUL SLAVIT.

NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff E. M. McCartt's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories.
